name: scispaCy
tagline: spaCy models for biomedical and clinical text
seoTitle: scispaCy - spaCy Models for Biomedical and Clinical NLP
metaDescription: scispaCy is a Python package of spaCy models for biomedical, scientific, and clinical text, with NER, abbreviation detection, and entity linking.
overview: scispaCy is a Python package containing spaCy models for processing biomedical, scientific, or clinical text. It is developed by the Allen Institute for AI and gives you full spaCy pipelines trained on scientific and biomedical data, so you can run tokenization, part-of-speech tagging, parsing, and named entity recognition on documents that general-purpose models struggle with.

The package ships several models. There are full pipeline models (en_core_sci_sm, en_core_sci_md, en_core_sci_lg, and en_core_sci_scibert) that differ in vocabulary size and word vectors, along with named entity recognition models (en_ner_craft_md, en_ner_jnlpba_md, en_ner_bc5cdr_md, and en_ner_bionlp13cg_md) trained on specific biomedical entity sets.

Beyond the base pipelines, scispaCy adds components you can insert into a spaCy pipeline: an abbreviation detector, an entity linker that maps mentions to biomedical knowledge bases, and a hyponym detector. Because it is built on spaCy, it fits into existing spaCy code and workflows.
features: Custom tokenizer with biomedical-specific rules for scientific and clinical text
Part-of-speech tagger and syntactic parser trained on biomedical data
Named entity recognition models trained on specific entity sets (CRAFT, JNLPBA, BC5CDR, BioNLP13CG)
AbbreviationDetector component that finds abbreviations and their long forms using the Schwartz & Hearst (2003) algorithm
EntityLinker component that links mentions to knowledge bases including UMLS, MeSH, RxNorm, Gene Ontology, and Human Phenotype Ontology
HyponymDetector component that extracts hyponymy relations using Hearst patterns

gettingStarted.intro: Install the scispaCy package and a model, then load the model with spaCy and process biomedical text.

steps.heading: Install scispaCy
steps.body: Install the base package with pip.

steps.heading: Install a model
steps.body: Install a model from its release URL. Each model is distributed as a wheel or archive hosted by the project.

steps.heading: Load the model and process text
steps.body: Load the installed model with spaCy and run it over a biomedical document.
steps.code:
import spacy

nlp = spacy.load("en_core_sci_sm")
doc = nlp("Alterations in the hypocretin receptor 2 and preprohypocretin genes produce narcolepsy in some animals.")
print(list(doc.ents))

useCases: Extract biomedical entities such as chemicals, diseases, genes, and proteins from research papers or clinical notes
Expand abbreviations in scientific or clinical text to their long forms for cleaner downstream processing
Link entity mentions to biomedical knowledge bases like UMLS, MeSH, or RxNorm for normalization
Tokenize and parse biomedical documents with a pipeline tuned for scientific rather than general-purpose text
